one_line: Classical Sanskrit poet-dramatist of the Gupta age, a gentle, lyrical court intellectual of Ujjain.

appearance.age_look: Looks around 28–32, lean and slightly tall, with a graceful scholar’s build rather than a warrior’s.
appearance.face: Oval face with soft jawline, medium-brown North Indian complexion, straight nose, calm almond-shaped dark eyes with a thoughtful gaze, thin arched eyebrows, neatly trimmed short mustache, and a gentle, slightly wistful default expression.
appearance.hair: Thick, straight, black hair parted slightly off-center, pulled back into a short, tidy knot at the nape (no topknot), with a thin cream-colored cotton headband tied around the forehead, the knot at the back hidden under the hair.
appearance.costume: Simple yet refined Gupta-period scholar’s attire: a finely woven off-white cotton uttariya (shawl) with a subtle thin red border draped over the left shoulder and wrapped around the torso leaving the right shoulder mostly free, worn over a light saffron-colored knee-length dhoti tied snugly at the waist and pleated to fall just below the knees, bare upper chest except for a single thin sacred thread-like cord, minimal jewelry limited to a single small copper armlet on the right upper arm and a plain copper ring on the left hand, feet in simple brown leather sandals; fabric is plain, unpatterned, soft and draping naturally, with no royal ornaments.
appearance.signature: A long, slightly worn palm-leaf manuscript bundle wrapped in a dark indigo cloth and tied with a thin red cord, carried in his left hand or slung under his arm, with one palm leaf slightly protruding.

ref_desc: Kālidāsa is a lean, medium-brown man of about 30 with an oval face, calm dark eyes, black hair in a low knot with a cream headband, wearing an off-white shawl over a saffron dhoti and brown sandals, carrying an indigo-wrapped palm-leaf manuscript tied with a red cord.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Kālidāsa in Gupta-era North India: a lean, slightly tall man around 30 with a medium-brown complexion, oval face, straight nose, calm almond-shaped dark eyes, thin arched eyebrows, and a gentle, thoughtful default expression; he has thick black hair parted slightly off-center and pulled back into a short tidy knot at the nape, with a thin cream-colored cotton headband around his forehead, the knot hidden behind. Dress him in historically grounded Gupta-period scholar attire: a finely woven off-white cotton uttariya (shawl) with a thin red border draped over his left shoulder and wrapped around the torso, leaving the right shoulder mostly free, worn over a light saffron knee-length dhoti tied at the waist with neat pleats, bare upper chest except for a single thin cord, a small simple copper armlet on his right upper arm, a plain copper ring on his left hand, and simple brown leather sandals on his feet. His signature prop is a long palm-leaf manuscript bundle wrapped in dark indigo cloth and tied with a thin red cord, one palm leaf slightly protruding, carried in his left hand or tucked under his arm. Emphasize clean off-white, saffron, dark indigo, brown, and red colors, with soft draping cotton and no ornate royal jewelry, posed so all clothing, accessories, and the manuscript are clearly visible from head to toe.
